t_ch: 你还能变成什么？
t_en: What else can you transform into?

t_ch: 不是跟你吹，只要我见过的有机生物，都可以变出来。可以是一头黄石牛，也可以是一个苹果。但体积不能差距特别大。
t_en: I'm not boasting. I can turn into any organic life form I've seen. It can be a bull, it can also be an apple, but the size difference can't be too large.

t_ch: 有机生物？那你能变有机苹果？
t_en: Organic life form? Then, can you turn into an organically produced apple?

t_ch: 什么有机苹果！我又不是农产品！好啊，我拿你当朋友，你竟然想吃我？
t_en: What are you even talking about? I'm not an agricultural product! Aeons! I treat you like a friend, and you treat me like food?

t_ch: 体型差距？你不能随地大小变？
t_en: Size difference? You can change how large you are at will?

t_ch: 是啊，很可惜，我不能随地…不对！什么大小变！能不能不要那么粗俗！
t_en: Yeah. Real shame. I can't change my... no! How large? Can you stop being so vulgar!

t_ch: 不过我确实也不太可能变成牛和苹果啦…要是总变成低智慧生物，我会变傻的。
t_en: But it is unlikely for me to turn into a cow or an apple... If I turn into a low-intelligence creature, I'll get kinda dumb, too.

t_ch: 况且，还是变成智慧生物更有意思一些～
t_en: Besides, it's more fun to become an intelligent creature~

t_ch: 你因为什么被关起来的？
t_en: Why are you locked up here?

t_ch: 他们说我盗用身份、诈骗、窃取机要，犯下了很多大罪…哎呀～真是的，这只是我的生物本能而已～
t_en: They said I've stolen identities, cheated people, stolen important intelligence, and committed lots of heinous crimes... Ah. Seriously, though, it's just my basic biological instinct.

t_ch: 把别人的生物本能定义为「犯罪」，然后进行一场煞有介事的审判…你们人类真的很蠢哦！
t_en: You define another lifeform's natural instincts as "crimes," then host a show trial about it... You humans are pretty silly!

t_ch: 你是哪种变形生物？
t_en: What kind of shapeshifter are you?

t_ch: 哪种变形生物？这个问题没什么意义嘛…我也不知道该从哪里开始回答…感觉我只能告诉你，我不是哪种变形生物。
t_en: What kind of shapeshifter? That's a meaningless question... I don't even know how to start answering that... Feels like I can only tell you that I'm not any kind of shapeshifter.

t_ch: 首先我不是异星水魈，丑八怪啊，没什么品味。蛤蟆一样的头身比，说起来话咕嘎咕嘎，浑身还湿漉漉的，光是描述我都觉得嘴巴里有股腥味。
t_en: First off, I'm not a Xenohydro. Those freaks have no taste. The body-to-head ratio of a toad, the gurgling way they talk... the wet, slimy body... I can taste how fishy they are from the description alone.

t_ch: 其次我不是红泥粘土人。那玩意儿就是坨融化的冰激凌，赶上下雨天搞不好都会被冲进下水道里，一点美感都没有。
t_en: Secondly, I'm not a Red Clayform. Those things are just like piles of melted ice cream. One rainy day and they'll end up getting washed into the sewers. No aesthetic at all.

t_ch: 至于我是什么…我就是我啦！就像你就是你那样！
t_en: As for what I am.... I'm just me! Just like you're just you!

t_ch: 下次再聊吧。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 哼～下次搞不好我都逃出幽囚狱了～
t_en: Hmph! Maybe I'll have escaped by then~

t_ch: 没什么事，下次见。
t_en: That's all. See you next time.

t_ch: 哼～下次搞不好我都逃出幽囚狱了～
t_en: Hmph! Maybe I'll have escaped by then~

t_ch: 想在长乐天搞套房子怎么这么难，不想卖就别卖，搞这么多限制干什么！
t_en: Why is it so hard to buy a place to live in Exalting Sanctum? They can just tell us straight if they don't want to sell. Why do they have so many restrictions instead!?

t_ch: 哎，什么购房资格，完全就是针对我们持明嘛。
t_en: "Property purchase eligibility." Heh. What a joke. It's just targeting us Vidyadhara.

t_ch: 难怪我听说这罗浮的龙尊有名无实…依我看，倒不如早日移民我们方壶去呢！
t_en: No wonder I heard people say that the Luofu's high elder is an incompetent figurehead... You know what? My kin here are betting off immigrating to the Fanghu!

t_ch: 说话不要这么偏激嘛。
t_en: You're being quite radical.

t_ch: 偏激？我哪里偏激了，不过是实话实说，你可别平白冤枉我。
t_en: Radical? I'm not radical! I'm just stating the truth! Don't go stick labels on me.

t_ch: 况且我这么说，也不过是希望能引起更多持明对这些问题的重视！
t_en: Besides, I'm saying this so more Vidyadhara can pay attention to these issues!

t_ch: 罗浮怎么你了！
t_en: What did the Luofu ever do to you!?

t_ch: 罗浮没有怎么我啊，我随口抱怨几句还不行了？
t_en: Nothing! Can't someone just complain about the world?

t_ch: 等等，你明明是个化外民，怎么还关心起罗浮的事来了？真是莫名其妙……
t_en: Wait, you're an Outworlder, so why would you care so much about the Luofu? I don't get you...

t_ch: 你好像对我有什么误解，我先申明，我并不是对罗浮有什么偏见。
t_en: You've misunderstood me, it seems. Let me be clear: I have nothing against the Luofu.

t_ch: 我只是觉得我们持明在罗浮生活有诸多不便，地衡司应当多顾及下我们的需求才是。
t_en: It's just that the Vidyadhara's daily lives on the Luofu have many inconveniences. The Realm-Keeping Commission should consider our needs more.

t_ch: 算了，这些事也急不得一时。倒是你，你是有什么事吗？
t_en: Ah, never mind. These things won't change overnight after all. But you... what brings you here?

t_ch: 你好奇什么？问就是了。
t_en: If you're curious about anything, just shoot it.

t_ch: 不便之处是指？
t_en: What do you mean by inconveniences?

t_ch: 生活嘛，无非是衣食住行，仙舟上都大差不差。水质不如我们方壶就不说了，这种客观因素就不怪他们了。
t_en: The general quality of life is pretty similar across all the Xianzhou ships. The water quality here is not as good as the Fanghu, where I'm from, but that's to be expected.

t_ch: 但单说这「住」吧，罗浮就真不如我们方壶了，我们那儿一蜕生就会分配一处居所。
t_en: But when it comes to housing, this place is simply awful. Whenever someone in the Fanghu has completed their hatching rebirth, they'll immediately get a residence for free.

t_ch: 可到了罗浮，买房还得走置业手续，说什么「你们持明每次蜕生完就把房产搁在那儿，纯属浪费」，嘁，那还不是他们地衡司做的蜕生登记服务不到位嘛！
t_en: But here on the Luofu, you've got to go through a whole lot of nasty real estate paperwork. The officials have lines like, "You Vidyadhara just abandon your real estate after each rebirth! What a waste of resources!" Tsk! They say that because their Realm-Keeping Commission is so incompetent at registering hatching rebirth!

t_ch: 你为什么说罗浮针对持明？
t_en: Why do you say the Luofu is targeting the Vidyadhara?

t_ch: 噢，我前不久看上了长乐天某座浮岛上的一套房子…唉，虽然价格不菲，但我也不缺钱，就想寻个位居要津、往来热闹的地方置业。
t_en: I've recently got my eyes on a property on a floating island in Exalting Sanctum. It's expensive, but I'm not short on money. I just want to be at a place with a lot of traffic and surrounded by amenities.

t_ch: 可他们却说我得提供连续三百年的健康证明和无罪业记录，才能证明我有不会突然蜕生搁置房产的风险。拜托，人家狐人只需要连续一百年的工作记录就够了。
t_en: But you know what? They say I've got to provide my health certificates and criminal conviction clearance across three centuries to prove I won't suddenly go through a hatching rebirth and leave the real estate I want to purchase suspended in limbo. Come on! Foxians only need a record of continuous employment for <unbreak>100</unbreak> years!

t_ch: 再说，蜕生这种事又不是我们能自主控制的，万一遇到点突发情况，蜕生完就丢失了全部记忆，谁还能记得每年要去体检的事啊？我看他们就是欺负老实人！
t_en: Besides, it's not like we can just control our own hatching rebirths. If we get into an accident, we don't get to keep any of our memories after the rebirth. How are we supposed to remember to do annual health checks? They're clearly just trying to get one over on us honest folk!

t_ch: 你好像不是罗浮本地人？
t_en: You are not a Luofu local, are you?

t_ch: 当然不是，我是从方壶来的。不知道你是否听说过「方寸烟海」的美名？
t_en: Of course not. I'm from the Fanghu. Have you heard of the famous Miniature Myriads?

t_ch: 没听过？唉，我料到了。哼，这些年方壶的风头尽被罗浮盖去了。
t_en: You haven't? *sigh* As I suspected. The Fanghu has been overshadowed by the Luofu these days!

t_ch: 但当年五龙远徙，还得数咱们方壶冱渊君一脉最为尊贵，是她代表持明与联盟签署了共治盟约。
t_en: But when the Five Dragons undertook their exodus it was our Glaciator Marum, the high elder of the Fanghu, who was the most venerable of them all. She was the one who signed the covenant with the Alliance on behalf of the Vidyadhara.

t_ch: 自此以后，咱们持明一族便为联盟镇压所谓的「丰饶仙迹」——啊对不住，该正儿八经地称为「寿瘟祸迹」才对——立下功劳无数。
t_en: Since then, we Vidyadhara have been working for the Alliance, and were decorated with many honors for suppressing the "Traces of Abundance"... Oh, I should call it "Plaguemark" as per the Alliance's request.

t_ch: 联盟感念功劳，便将方壶仙舟赠给咱们自治啦。冱渊君也真不愧是万世贤君，将方壶治理得繁荣昌盛，颇有再兴先祖遗业的势头呢。
t_en: As a reward, the Alliance granted us the Xianzhou Fanghu to rule as an autonomous ship. The Glaciator Marum continues to prove herself as a capable ruler, and I expected nothing less from a revered scion of a venerable lineage! Her establishments almost rival the glorious days of our bygone history!

t_ch: 当然了，要论烟火气息，方壶确实不如罗浮这般热闹。咱们做生意的求财，无非想沾点烟火气。
t_en: I have to admit that the Fanghu is not as lively as the Luofu, and that's why I came here. I'm a merchant, and we chase after this bustling atmosphere.

t_ch: 要不是在这儿有商机，我才不想过来呢。你瞧这星槎，造型如此无趣，和我们方壶比可差远了！
t_en: I wouldn't have wanted to come at all if it weren't for the business opportunities. Just look at this starskiff! Its shape is so dull and plain, and it's got way less aesthetic than the ones on the Fanghu!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了…
t_en: That's all I wanted to ask...

t_ch: 行，我还要在罗浮待一段时间，有缘再见吧{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}。
t_en: Sure, I'll be staying in the Luofu for a while. See you around, {F#young lady}{M#young man}.

t_ch: 好，我也有事要忙，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}咱们有空再聊。
t_en: Sure, I have my business to attend to. {F#Young lady}{M#Young man}. Let's talk again next time.

t_ch: 登云群阁这地段确实不错，无论投资还是自住都是笔好买卖，好好的搞什么限购呢……
t_en: The Skyward Pavilions neighborhood is a great place to invest or live in. If only the properties there were not limited for purchase...

t_ch: 我看这罗浮…哎，不提也罢，反正不是什么安逸地儿，哼哼，我们持明不待也罢！
t_en: If you ask me, I'd say the Luofu... Well, forget it. It's just not a place where you'd call home. We Vidyadhara are better off elsewhere!

t_ch: 咦，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}，怎么又是你，你找我是有什么事吗？
t_en: Hey, it's you again, {F#young lady}{M#young man}. What brings you here this time?

t_ch: 我有些好奇…
t_en: I'm just a bit curious...

t_ch: 算了，我没什么想问的了。
t_en: Forget it. I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 今时往日，物是人非，只剩喧闹依旧…呵呵，不好意思，这不过是一个老东西怀古伤今的自言自语罢了。怎么，有事要找我吗？
t_en: So much has changed, yet this alley remains the same... Hehe, please don't mind this old man's sentimental remarks. How can I help you?

t_ch: 我想和你随便聊聊。
t_en: I'm just here for a casual chat.

t_ch: 我现在没有手、也没有脚，只剩一张会说话的嘴了…聊吧，你想聊什么来着？
t_en: A casual chat, of course. Now that I've lost all my limbs, all I can do is chat... So, what would you want to talk about?

t_ch: 要帮你安装脖子以下的部分吗？
t_en: Do you need me to restore your body below your neck?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，我知道你心地善良，不过倒也不必为我操心。
t_en: Hahaha, thank you, kindhearted traveler, but don't worry about that.

t_ch: 不知道你对银河的历史有多少了解，在久远的过去，曾经有过一场寰宇浩劫，那是由无机生命的「帝皇」鲁珀特发起的战争。
t_en: I wonder how much you know about the history of the Cosmos. Once upon a time, there was a catastrophe, a war started by Rubert, the emperor of inorganic beings.

t_ch: 战争旷日持久，无数生灵湮灭…后来机械帝国覆灭，人们的恐惧却从未停止，人们将能言会道的智慧机械视作是潜在的威胁。
t_en: The war lasted for eons and consumed countless lives... Even after the downfall of the Machine Empire, the fear it aroused never ceased, as people saw intelligent machines as a potential threat.

t_ch: 我的玉兆模组上几乎没有保留那个时代的数据，仅仅留下了一些修改痕迹——据说是为了抹除寰宇中「反有机方程」的可能性。
t_en: My jade abacus module retains almost no data from that era except for a few traces of modifications, presumably to eradicate the very existence of the Anti-Organic Equation in the universe.

t_ch: 这与你只剩下一颗头有关系吗？
t_en: Does this have anything to do with the fact that all that's left of you is a head?

t_ch: 我原本不想说得太直白。
t_en: Fine, I didn't want to be so blunt at first...

t_ch: 只剩下一颗头的我对所有人毫无威胁，你们舒服，我也舒服。
t_en: With only a head left, I won't pose a threat to anyone. You're happy about it, and so am I.

t_ch: 我看现在还是有很多无机生命…
t_en: But there are still a lot of inorganic beings around...

t_ch: 我听说过天才俱乐部的「机械贵族」螺丝咕姆。
t_en: Well, I've heard of Screwllum, the "mechanical aristocrat," of the Genius Society.

t_ch: 人们崇敬他尊敬他，但又有多少人担忧着他是下个「帝皇」呢？
t_en: People gaze upon him in awe and reverence, but how many of them are actually worried that he might become the next "Emperor"?

t_ch: 假如我装上了躯干与手脚，你想象不到的麻烦就会接踵而来。
t_en: If I had my body and my limbs, I'd be immediately facing troubles beyond your wildest imagination.

t_ch: 我只是想平静地生活在金人巷里，没有手脚也没有什么不便，哈哈哈。
t_en: Now I just want to live a peaceful life in this Aurum Alley, and losing my limbs is an acceptable price to pay. Hahaha.

t_ch: 过去的金人巷到底是有多繁华呢？
t_en: How prosperous was this alley before?

t_ch: 你知道的，我只能描述我目所能及的感受。
t_en: As you know, I can only describe what I can experience as far as my eyes can see.

t_ch: 我想想…那时这里充满了金人，都是为了自己的主人来金人巷购物取货的，街道拥堵不堪，几乎没有落脚的地方。
t_en: Let me see... Back then, this alley was alive with aurumatons shopping and picking up goods for their masters. The alley was so crowded that you could barely get in.

t_ch: 游客来回折腾一趟，累得气喘吁吁的，不得不找个地方坐下喝两口茶饮，买些小吃，休息休息…交流着一天的收获。
t_en: All the tourists who went there would be so exhausted that they had to buy some drinks and snacks... while taking a break and exchanging their stories.

t_ch: 那时的会长告诉我「你要好好记住眼前的风景，金人巷商会的使命就是要将这样的繁荣一直延续下去」……
t_en: At that time, my predecessor told me, "Remember the view you are seeing now. The Aurum Alley Merchant Guild is dedicated to continuing such prosperity for generations to come." Well...

t_ch: 哎，任何地方都有世事变迁，想要延续那些日子并不容易。
t_en: But alas, things do change. It's not easy to continue those good, old days.

t_ch: 我是不是什么问题都可以问你？
t_en: Am I allowed to ask you any questions?

t_ch: 你想听标准的回答，或者是「别的版本」？
t_en: Would you like to hear my "official" answer, or "another version"?

t_ch: 标准回答。
t_en: The official answer.

t_ch: 是的，您可以向我提出任何问题，只要它们符合社会准则和道德标准，并且不违反任何法律规定。
t_en: Yes, you are free to ask me any questions you want as long as they are in line with social norms, ethical standards, and legal regulations.

t_ch: 我虽然*不是*聊天机巧，但我将尽力回答您的问题或为您提供有用的信息和建议。
t_en: While I'm NOT designed for chatting, I'll do my best to answer your questions or provide you with helpful information and advice.

t_ch: 「别的版本」。
t_en: "Another version."

t_ch: 你能问到很多「标准回答」，但是它们不一定是你要的答案。
t_en: I can provide you with many "official answers," but they may not be the answers you seek.

t_ch: 我的回答可能来自书籍、邸报、网络、以及各种公共领域的信息，你可以相信我，就像你相信书籍、邸报和网络一样。
t_en: You can trust me as much as you can trust books, newspapers, the Internet, and various common fields of information, since that's where I draw information to formulate my reply.

t_ch: 但是数据库不能代替思考，这道理不用我告诉你，你也明白吧。
t_en: But having a database doesn't mean you don't have to think. I'm sure you don't need me to tell you that.

t_ch: 不打扰你啦。
t_en: I'll leave you alone, then.

t_ch: 慢走不送。
t_en: See you.

t_ch: 不打扰你啦。
t_en: I'll leave you alone, then.

t_ch: 慢走不送。
t_en: See you.

t_ch: 商业顾问来了，我们开始工作吧。
t_en: The business consultant is here. Let's get to work.

t_ch: 处理金人巷事务
t_en: Take care of the affairs in Aurum Alley

t_ch: 有你帮忙，真是金人巷之幸。
t_en: It's a blessing to have you here in Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 我想和你随便聊聊。
t_en: I'm just here for a casual chat.

t_ch: 我现在没有手、也没有脚，只剩一张会说话的嘴了…聊吧，你想聊什么来着？
t_en: A casual chat, of course. Now that I've lost all my limbs, all I can do is chat... So, what would you want to talk about?

t_ch: 要帮你安装脖子以下的部分吗？
t_en: Do you need me to restore your body below your neck?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，我知道你心地善良，不过倒也不必为我操心。
t_en: Hahaha, thank you, kindhearted traveler, but don't worry about that.

t_en: The war lasted for eons and consumed countless lives... Even after the downfall of the Machine Empire, the fear it aroused never ceased, as people saw intelligent machines as a potential threat.

t_en: Does this have anything to do with the fact that all that's left of you is a head?

t_ch: 我原本不想说得太直白。
t_en: Fine, I didn't want to be so blunt at first...

t_ch: 只剩下一颗头的我对所有人毫无威胁，你们舒服，我也舒服。
t_en: With only a head left, I won't pose a threat to anyone. You're happy about it, and so am I.

t_en: But there are still a lot of inorganic beings around...

t_ch: 我听说过天才俱乐部的「天才贵族」螺丝咕姆。
t_en: Well, I've heard of Screwllum, the "Genius Aristocrat" of the Genius Society.

t_ch: 人们崇敬他尊敬他，但又有多少人担忧着他是下个「帝皇」呢？
t_en: People gaze upon him in awe and reverence, but how many of them are actually worried that he might become the next "Emperor"?

t_ch: 假如我装上了躯干与手脚，你想象不到的麻烦就会接踵而来。
t_en: If I had my body and my limbs, I'd be immediately facing troubles beyond your wildest imagination.

t_ch: 我只是想平静地生活在金人巷里，没有手脚也没有什么不便，哈哈哈。
t_en: Now I just want to live a peaceful life in this Aurum Alley, and losing my limbs is an acceptable price to pay. Hahaha.

t_ch: 过去的金人巷到底是有多繁华呢？
t_en: How prosperous was this alley before?

t_ch: 你知道的，我只能描述我目所能及的感受。
t_en: As you know, I can only describe what I can experience as far as my eyes can see.

t_ch: 我想想…那时这里充满了金人，都是为了自己的主人来金人巷购物取货的，街道拥堵不堪，几乎没有落脚的地方。
t_en: Let me see... Back then, this alley was alive with aurumatons shopping and picking up goods for their masters. The alley was so crowded that you could barely get in.

t_ch: 游客来回折腾一趟，累得气喘吁吁的，不得不找个地方坐下喝两口茶饮，买些小吃，休息休息…交流着一天的收获。
t_en: All the tourists who went there would be so exhausted that they had to buy some drinks and snacks... while taking a break and exchanging their stories.

t_ch: 那时的会长告诉我「你要好好记住眼前的风景，金人巷商会的使命就是要将这样的繁荣一直延续下去」……
t_en: At that time, my predecessor told me, "Remember the view you are seeing now. The Aurum Alley Merchant Guild is dedicated to continuing such prosperity for generations to come." Well...

t_ch: 哎，任何地方都有世事变迁，想要延续那些日子并不容易。
t_en: But alas, things do change. It's not easy to continue those good, old days.

t_ch: 我是不是什么问题都可以问你？
t_en: Am I allowed to ask you any questions?

t_ch: 你想听标准的回答，或者是「别的版本」？
t_en: Would you like to hear my "official" answer, or "another version"?

t_ch: 标准回答。
t_en: The official answer.

t_ch: 是的，您可以向我提出任何问题，只要它们符合社会准则和道德标准，并且不违反任何法律规定。
t_en: Yes, you are free to ask me any questions you want as long as they are in line with social norms, ethical standards, and legal regulations.

t_ch: 我虽然*不是*聊天机巧，但我将尽力回答您的问题或为您提供有用的信息和建议。
t_en: While I'm NOT designed for chatting, I'll do my best to answer your questions or provide you with helpful information and advice.

t_ch: 「别的版本」。
t_en: "Another version."

t_ch: 你能问到很多「标准回答」，但是它们不一定是你要的答案。
t_en: I can provide you with many "official answers," but they may not be the answers you seek.

t_ch: 我的回答可能来自书籍、邸报、网络、以及各种共领域的信息，你可以相信我，就像你相信书籍、邸报和网络一样。
t_en: You can trust me as much as you can trust books, newspapers, the Internet, and various common fields of information.

t_ch: 但是数据库不能代替思考，这道理不用我告诉你，你也明白吧。
t_en: But having a database doesn't mean you don't have to think. I'm sure you don't need me to tell you that.

t_ch: 不打扰你啦。
t_en: I'll leave you alone, then.

t_ch: 慢走不送。
t_en: See you.

t_ch: 不打扰你啦。
t_en: I'll leave you alone, then.

t_ch: 慢走不送。
t_en: See you.

t_ch: 我想和你随便聊聊。
t_en: I'm just here for a casual chat.

t_ch: 不打扰你啦。
t_en: I'll leave you alone, then.

t_ch: 处理金人巷事务
t_en: Take care of the affairs in Aurum Alley

t_ch: 我想和你随便聊聊。
t_en: I'm just here for a casual chat.

t_ch: 不打扰你啦。
t_en: I'll leave you alone, then.

t_ch: 最近一切还好吗？
t_en: How's everything lately?

t_ch: 好得很，这都是因为你和素裳姑娘的努力。要是没有你们，我这金人巷啊…还真不知道会何去何从呢。
t_en: I'm doing great because of you and Sushang. Without you two, who knew what would happen to our Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 往后常来这里玩玩吧，金人巷也是日新月异，变得我都快不认得了。
t_en: Do come here often to have fun! Things in the Aurum Alley change so fast that I can barely keep up.

t_ch: 也许再过一段时间，它会变成一个连你都会觉得惊讶的好地方吧。
t_en: Perhaps in a little while, it will transform into a place so wonderful that it will blow you away.

t_ch: 也许到了那个时候，金人巷就再也不需要我了。但那样更好，真的。
t_en: When the time comes, I might no longer be needed here. That's not a bad thing though.

t_ch: 不管公司想做什么，金人巷都应该做出积极改变。请问今天是有什么工作计划吗？
t_en: Regardless of the IPC's wishes, positive changes need to be made to Aurum Alley. Do you have any work plans for today?

t_ch: 处理金人巷事务
t_en: Take care of the affairs in Aurum Alley

t_ch: 金人巷复兴计划还没完成，我们继续加油吧。
t_en: There's still more work to do to revitalize Aurum Alley. Let's give it our all.

t_ch: 可以随便聊聊吗？
t_en: Can I have a casual chat with you?

t_ch: 当然可以，不过我不像会长那样有什么见解，如果不嫌弃的话……
t_en: Sure, but I'm not as erudite as the president, and if you don't mind...

t_ch: 小秘书在金人巷挺受欢迎的？
t_en: You are popular in Aurum Alley, right?

t_ch: 哪里哪里，都是金人巷的商户们很照顾我，我哪里担当得起……
t_en: Yeah, I guess so, thanks to the kind merchants in the alley...

t_ch: 商会秘书这份工作，主要就是和各种人打交道，久而久之就和大家熟悉起来了。
t_en: Being a secretary means dealing with a wide variety of people, and we've become acquainted over time.

t_ch: 会长说「待人真诚」一次不难，要始终「待人真诚」才是难事。
t_en: According to the president, it's easy to be sincere with someone once, but it's hard to be sincere all the time.

t_ch: 你看，现在我也受到了大家的照顾，这也许就是「待人真诚」奏效了。
t_en: As you see, everyone has been nice to me. Perhaps this is the result of my sincerity.

t_ch: 小秘书怎么看待星际和平公司？
t_en: What do you think of the Interastral Peace Corporation?

t_ch: 你要想听我说，就别告诉其他商会的同事呀……
t_en: Well, I can be frank with you, but please keep my words a secret from my colleagues...

t_ch: 同事们都觉得公司就像是「只会入侵和破坏的商业野兽」…但是，我每天都用着公司的产品，也听说过公司的故事，我总觉得这之间很矛盾。
t_en: In their eyes, IPC is no different than a ruthless beast that leaves only devastation in its wake in the business world... However, as a customer who uses IPC products every day and knows about its origins, I don't think my colleagues' claims really make sense.

t_ch: 想来想去，我认为「奸诈」、「勇敢」这些评价人的词语，放在公司上都不对。
t_en: I don't think words that are used to describe people, such as "treacherous" or "courageous," can be used to describe a corporation.

t_ch: 公司并不是「一个活生生的人」，也不会只有「一张面孔」，公司显然是一种更为复杂的东西，既不是猛兽，也不是善人…你理解吗？
t_en: Obviously, a corporation is not a "living being," nor does it have only one "face." It's something more complicated. It's not a ruthless beast or a righteous person... if you know what I mean.

t_ch: 换言之，你没有评价？
t_en: So, you don't have any remarks on IPC?

t_ch: 如果从商业上来评价的话，公司毫无疑问是成功的。
t_en: Well, from a business point of view, the IPC is definitely a success.

t_ch: …剩下来的部分我只用说，我还不太了解公司，不能评价。
t_en: ...As for other aspects, I don't know what to say as I don't know much about it.

t_ch: 你是在说「公司拟人化」？
t_en: Are you saying that your colleagues view the IPC in the same way they view people?

t_ch: 拟人化？你这么说也没有错。
t_en: In the same way they view people? Yeah, in a sense that's what they've been doing.

t_ch: 同事们在谈到公司的时候，总是把它当做东方启行或是路易斯•弗莱明。
t_en: Whenever they talk about the IPC, they do it in a way as if it were Dongfang Qixing or Louis Fleming.

t_ch: 对外宣传时，「公司」是宣传部门；采购物资时，「公司」又是采购部门；对外讨债时，「公司」又是债务部门……
t_en: But in fact, the IPC plays different roles when it comes to advertising, procurement, and debt collection...

t_ch: 所以我想，只是用「代表人物」人来评价「公司」，总是有些以偏概全。
t_en: So, I think it's biased to evaluate a corporation based solely on its "representatives."

t_ch: 小秘书不会一直待在这吧？
t_en: You're not standing here all the time, are you?

t_ch: 你当然知道我为什么会站在这里——这不是为了方便你来找到我嘛！
t_en: You know why I'm standing here — I'm standing here so that you can find me at any time!

t_ch: 商会秘书本应该是坐在办公室里处理事务的工作，不知道什么时候开始，我就没有什么机会坐下来了。
t_en: I'm supposed to sit in my office dealing with business work, but I don't remember how long I've been standing here without a chance to sit down.

t_ch: 金人巷商会同事们经常来找我的，会给我带些小零食，都挺好吃的。
t_en: My colleagues in Aurum Alley often come to me and bring me snacks, and they are tasty.

t_ch: 哎，希望我的办公椅没有被收走吧。
t_en: *Sigh* I hope they haven't taken the chair out of my office.

t_ch: 我有事就先走了。
t_en: Now if you'll excuse me.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，辛苦啦，我这边也有别的事要处理。有什么问题，可以随时来找我。
t_en: Yeah, sure, I've got some work to do, too. Feel free to come to me if you have any questions.

t_ch: 我有事就先走了。
t_en: Now if you'll excuse me.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，辛苦啦，我这边也有别的事要处理。有什么问题，可以随时来找我。
t_en: Yeah, sure, I've got some work to do, too. Feel free to come to me if you have any questions.

t_ch: 欢迎光临！我们家卖的都是地道的罗浮小吃，都是刚出锅的，新鲜得很！
t_en: Welcome! I only sell authentic Luofu snacks! Freshly baked!

t_ch: 客官，要来尝尝正宗的罗浮小吃吗？绝对让您回味无穷！
t_en: Want to try some local Luofu snacks, customer? You'll love them!

t_ch: 你都卖些什么？
t_en: What have you got for sale?

t_ch: 我现在没什么想买的。
t_en: I don't have an appetite now.

t_ch: 谢谢光临！
t_en: Thank you for stopping by!

t_ch: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
t_en: Has business been going well lately?

t_ch: 那是越来越顺了啊！金人巷的游客越来越多，我们家地道的罗浮小吃，现在可是卖得越来越好了啊！
t_en: My business is booming! More and more tourists are flocking the Aurum Alley, and our authentic Luofu snacks are selling like hotcakes!

t_ch: 还有你帮我找来的评书相声，也颇受欢迎。有些客人原本不是来吃小吃的，就是被店里的相声勾住了，只好坐下也买些小吃，生意更好做了。
t_en: The storytelling and crosstalk comedy sessions you arranged for me are well-received too. Some people sit down and order something to eat just because they are drawn to the performances. That makes our business even better.

t_ch: 欢迎光临，本店药品均有丹鼎司备案，品质上乘，童叟无欺，请随意挑选。
t_en: Welcome! All our medicines are licensed by the Alchemy Commission and are of the best quality. Please, take your time.

t_ch: 客官您好，请问有什么需要的吗？
t_en: Greetings, customer. What do you need?

t_ch: 你这都卖些什么？
t_en: What have you got for sale here, then?

t_ch: 暂时没什么需要的了。
t_en: I don't need anything for now.

t_ch: 感谢您的光临，再见！
t_en: Thank you for your patronage. See you next time!

t_ch: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
t_en: Has business been going well lately?

t_ch: 挺顺利的，现在生意也越来越好了。化外民游客说是来这边观光，结果没少在我这里买药。
t_en: It's going pretty well. Although the Outworlder tourists are here to travel, they contribute a lot to my medicine sales.

t_ch: 我店里炮制的药丸主打一个「方便易保存」，有客人看我气色好，以为我有什么调养的药物让我推荐…其实我只是精神气好，皮肤就跟着好了。
t_en: The pills I make are known for being easy to consume and store. Some customers ask me to recommend them some supplements after seeing my flawless skin... Well, my skin is in good condition just because I'm in a good space mentally.

t_ch: 欢迎光临，需要我为您介绍一下我们的特色菜吗？
t_en: Welcome! May I interest you with our specialties?

t_ch: 你这儿都有什么菜？
t_en: What specialties do you have?

t_ch: 算了，我现在没胃口。
t_en: Forget it. I don't have an appetite now.

t_ch: 感谢光临，期待您下次再来！
t_en: Thank you for your patronage. Come back next time!

t_ch: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
t_en: Has business been going well lately?

t_ch: 最近蛮好的。不过我们开店做生意，还是不能满足于当下。既然咱们金人巷都越变越好了，那我这摊生意也要越做越好才行。
t_en: It's been quite well lately. However, when it comes to running a business, we can't rest on our laurels. Since the Aurum Alley is improving, my business should do the same too.

t_ch: 说起来，之前不是拜托你搞了些丰饶孽物的食材…嗯，材料吗？从此我彻底斩断了念想，一门心思投入到传统菜品里，差异化才是我的特色。
t_en: Speaking of which, do you remember that I asked you to get me some Abominations of Abundance... ingredients? I've made up my mind to focus on traditional dishes. Differentiation is what makes my place special.

t_ch: 结果客人们也很赏光，我感觉现在的美馔阁比以前更加有魅力，哈哈哈，这都得感谢你。
t_en: As it turns out, my customers love the food. Delicacy Pavilion now seems to be more charming than before. Hahaha, I owe this to you.

t_ch: …客人是来修什么东西的？敝店能打包票，将您的机巧修复如初。
t_en: ...Need something fixed? We guarantee that your ingenium will be good as new.

t_ch: 不过要是化外民的科技，我得看看是哪个世界的。
t_en: Of course, if it's outworlder technology, it'll depend on the world...

t_ch: 我不是来修东西的。
t_en: I didn't come to fix anything.

t_ch: 客人，我只会修东西。
t_en: But I only fix things, dear customer.

t_ch: 我的心能恢复如初吗？
t_en: Can you fix my broken heart?

t_ch: 客人，我不会谜语。
t_en: I'm not good at riddles, dear customer.

t_ch: 既然客人不是来修机巧偃偶的，我便继续忙了。
t_en: If you're not here to fix your ingenium marionette, then I'll get back to work.

t_ch: 你甚至都没有看我一眼。
t_en: You didn't even look at me.

t_ch: 这就是匠人吗？
t_en: Are all craftsmen so dedicated to their work?

t_ch: …我不擅长闲聊，客人有什么事吗？
t_en: ...I'm not good at small talk, dear customer. What can I do for you?

t_ch: 想随便聊聊。
t_en: Just a casual chat.

t_ch: 请恕我拒绝。
t_en: Then please allow me to refuse.

t_ch: 我没什么事了。
t_en: I have nothing else.

t_ch: 慢走不送。
t_en: See you.

t_ch: 对了，这个东西你拿上，送你的。
t_en: By the way, take this. A gift for you.

t_ch: …我不擅长闲聊，客人有什么事吗？
t_en: ...I'm not good at small talk, dear customer. What can I do for you?

t_ch: 请恕我拒绝。
t_en: Then please allow me to refuse.

t_ch: 我没什么事了。
t_en: I have nothing else.

t_ch: 慢走不送。
t_en: See you.

t_ch: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
t_en: Has business been going well lately?

t_ch: 还可以吧。多谢关心。
t_en: Not bad. Thanks for asking.

t_ch: …你好，请问你是有事找我吗？
t_en: ...Good day. Do you have something for me?

t_ch: 真是令人印象深刻的演出。
t_en: That was an impressive performance.

t_ch: 能得到听众的赏识，是我作为乐师的荣幸。
t_en: It's an honor for a minstrel to be appreciated by her audience.

t_ch: 请问我可以在哪里打赏呢？
t_en: Could you tell me how I should tip you?

t_ch: 呵呵，我只是在义演，不需要打赏。您愿意驻足聆听，这对我来说就应足够了。
t_en: Oh, I'm just performing for charity, so tips will be unnecessary. Your willingness to stop and listen to my performance is more than enough for me.

t_ch: 你的噪声吵到我了。
t_en: Such noise is really a nuisance.

t_ch: 噢，是这样吗…那真是不好意思了。
t_en: Oh... is that so...? I'm so sorry about that.

t_ch: 我看其他的听众都挺享受其中的，没想到您会如此不堪其扰……
t_en: All the other audience are immersed in my music. I didn't expect it to distract you so much...

t_ch: 我打算继续精进自己的演奏技术，到时请您再多多批评。
t_en: Looks like I still need to hone my performance skills. Please, do come back next time.

t_ch: …请问一下，您能从刚才的演奏中收获平静吗？
t_en: ...May I ask you a question? Did my performance soothe your mind?

t_ch: 感觉内心很平静。
t_en: Yes, I've found my inner peace.

t_ch: 那真是太好了。
t_en: Glad to know that.

t_ch: 我内心依旧焦躁不安。
t_en: No, I'm still feeling anxious.

t_ch: …看来音乐能做到的事也是有限的。
t_en: ...It seems music is not the remedy for all troubled souls.

t_ch: 要是我的演奏能帮您抚平些许不安就好了。
t_en: If only my performance could calm you down, even if just a little...

t_ch: 自从建木之灾发生之后，我就想着自己应该做些什么…可是我既不会维护秩序，我也不会看病施诊，我只会拨弄乐器。
t_en: I've been thinking about what I should do since the disaster at the Ambrosial Arbor... but I don't know how to maintain order or treat the wounded... All I can do is play my instruments.

t_ch: 过去，短生种朋友曾经告诉我，我的音乐让她感觉到内心平静。于是我想，我应该走上街头去做我能做到的事，哪怕只是抚平人们心中的情绪，我就满足了。
t_en: A short-life species friend once told me that my music had soothed her soul. So I thought, perhaps I should do whatever I could? In the end, I decided to perform in public, hoping that my performance would also soothe the souls of those in distress.

t_ch: 当然可以，您想问什么呢？
t_en: Of course. What would you like to know?

t_ch: 请问这是什么乐器呢？
t_en: What is this instrument?

t_ch: 这是一种叫作「编钟」的乐器，据说在仙舟起航之初就已经存在了。
t_en: This is an ancient musical instrument called "chimes," which existed even as early as when this Xianzhou ship began its voyage.

t_ch: 原本的编钟钟架高大，甚至有长短不同的两堵立面相交，演奏复杂乐曲时，还得依赖多人协同演奏…不过你看，现在我一人也能将它带到长乐天来。
t_en: In ancient times, chime sets had towering frames resembling high walls, and playing complex music would require multiple minstrels... But they've been improved over time, and now I can play them alone in Exalting Sanctum.

t_ch: 虽然外观有所改变，但是器乐原理是一致的。编钟的钟体小，音调就高；编钟的钟体大，音调就低。再加之敲击技巧不同，每个钟体还能发出不同的乐音……
t_en: Although their appearance has changed dramatically, the principle remains the same. Smaller-sized chimes have a higher pitch, while larger-sized ones have a lower pitch. In addition, when struck in different ways, these chimes can produce different tones...

t_ch: 我一不小心就说太多了，您一时半会儿也很难理解。不过没关系，音乐本就应该是所听即所得，您只需要用心聆听，无需其他准备。
t_en: Oh, please forgive me for overwhelming you with my own interest. But don't worry about those technical details, because music is meant to be heard, not analyzed.

t_ch: 你一直都有演出吗？
t_en: Have you been performing all the time?

t_ch: 我过去没有做过路演…说来不好意思，我自知水平有限，我也是拿出不少勇气，才下定决心来到这里当众演出的。
t_en: No... I've never performed in public in the past... I know I'm not a skilled minstrel, so it takes me a lot of time to pluck up the courage to perform in public.

t_ch: 放心，你的演奏水平很高。
t_en: Fret not, you are indeed a skilled minstrel.

t_ch: 是吗？能听到这样的褒奖真是太好了。
t_en: Oh, is that so? Thank you so much for your compliment.

t_ch: 确实，你的演奏水平有待提升。
t_en: That's true. Your performance skills still have much room for improvement.

t_ch: …我也是这样认为的。要是能坚持路演下去，我的水平肯定能提升吧。
t_en: ...That's what I thought, too. So, if I continue to perform in public like this, I will be able to improve my skills.

t_ch: 我断断续续地练习了约五十年，才刚摸到编钟的门槛。
t_en: I've been practicing chimes for about fifty years, and now I'm just starting to get the hang of it.

t_ch: 有时我在想，只要是追求艺术，长生种有再多的时间也不够吧。
t_en: Sometimes I can't help but wonder, even for us long-life species, do we really have enough time to achieve our goals in our artistic pursuits?

t_ch: 我不打扰了，请继续演奏。
t_en: I won't bother you any further. Keep on playing.

t_ch: 慢慢走，慢慢听。
t_en: Take your time, and listen.

t_ch: 又和您见面了。希望我的音乐也能让你感觉到内心平静。
t_en: Hello again. I hope my music will soothe your mind.

t_ch: 可以再和您随便聊聊吗？
t_en: Can we have another casual chat?

t_ch: 我不打扰了，请继续演奏。
t_en: I won't bother you any further. Keep on playing.

t_ch: 这里就是仙舟「罗浮」啊，果然百闻不如一见……
t_en: So this is the Xianzhou Luofu... Ah, what an eye-opener...

t_ch: 呃，你好？我才来仙舟不久，冒昧问一句…阁下是？
t_en: Hmm... hello? I've only been here for a short while... May I know your name?

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你、你就是老师在说过的那位…我的{F#师妹}{M#师弟}！
t_en: Y—You are my... my {F#junior soror}{M#junior frater}! Master told me about you!

t_ch: 噢对了，刚刚太激动了，忘记做自我介绍了，有些失礼，抱歉抱歉。
t_en: Oh! Where are my manners? I'm sorry. I was got a bit too carried away.

t_ch: 在下是博识学会的学士克利欧，师从陶德·雷奥登老师。之前老师曾多次写信催我来仙舟，还说为我找了位{F#师妹}{M#师弟}——百闻不如一见，没想到就是你啊。
t_en: I'm Cleo, a scholar of the Intelligentsia Guild, under the tutelage of Master Todd Riordan. Master has written to me many times urging me to come to the Xianzhou ship, and he told me that he had found a {F#junior soror}{M#junior frater} for me. Just when I was wondering what my new peer would be like, you appeared before my eyes!

t_ch: 奇怪的是…等我收到信没多久，陶德老师就再也联系不上了。
t_en: Oddly enough... I lost contact with Master Todd shortly after I received his last letter.

t_ch: 我来仙舟数日，也一直没找到他。哎…{NICKNAME}，你最近还有见过老师吗？
t_en: So I came to the Xianzhou ship looking for him, but to no avail... Have you met with Master lately, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: （向克利欧隐瞒陶德的下落）
t_en: (Don't tell Cleo what happened to Todd.)

t_ch: 这可难办了，学会那边还要办理外派项目交接的手续，现在正流转到他那儿呢。
t_en: Now this is terrible. One of the Guild's exchange programs needs his signature before it can officially kick off.

t_ch: 该不会是为了调查罗浮的灾异而去了某些危险的地方吧？这老头…还是那么让人不省心啊。
t_en: Wait, he hasn't gone somewhere dangerous to investigate the source of the calamity on the Luofu, has he? That old man... He just won't stop getting himself into trouble.

t_ch: （向克利欧说出陶德的真相）
t_en: (Tell Cleo what happened to Todd.)

t_ch: …关于「长生」——咳咳，这里人多嘴杂，我们还是小点声说。
t_en: ...Well, let's find a more private place to talk about "immortality."

t_ch: 这个「课题」，老师自打来了仙舟以后，就力排众议非要重新开题，但我资历尚浅，当时我是有些无法理解。
t_en: Ever since Master came to the Xianzhou ship, he had been pushing to resume this subject, despite the opposition from other scholars. At that time, I was still an inexperienced scholar and didn't quite understand his decision.

t_ch: 毕竟学会里已经尝试过无数次，摊到我们课题上的经费捉襟见肘，人手也远远不够…怎么想都是不可能完成的事。
t_en: After all, the Guild had tried many times, but to no avail. We had only meager funds and scholars for the subject... I was convinced that the subject was impossible.

t_ch: 没想到老师他还是…虽然代价如此沉重，但无论如何，也让他摸到了那个「课题」的秘密，这大抵称得上是，半只脚踏入「智识」的门槛内了吧？
t_en: But I didn't expect Master to... In the end, he glimpsed the secrets of truth, even though it cost him dearly... Perhaps he eventually entered the threshold of "Erudition"?

t_ch: 说实话，我现在反倒是有些羡慕老师了…像我这种庸才，这辈子或许都只能远远地望着「智识」的大门却无法靠近半步……
t_en: To be honest, his fate is enviable to me... Will a mediocrity like me ever achieve anything as he did? Maybe I won't even have a chance to approach the threshold of "Erudition" in my life...

t_ch: 你看这封信——老师当时还一直催我过来呢，没想到我前脚刚下星槎，老师他就……
t_en: I still remember how he urged me to come here in his last letter. Who would have thought that by the time I arrived, he...

t_ch: …抱歉，因为老师的事，我实在是没什么心情闲聊了。你若是没什么重要的事，还是先请回吧，容我一个人静静。
t_en: ...I'm sorry, but I'm not in the mood for pleasantries right now. If there's nothing important to discuss, please leave me alone for a while.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，是你啊…本来想着来仙舟还能多个{F#学妹}{M#学弟}，我们的课题组也算后继有人啦。
t_en: It's you, {NICKNAME}... I initially thought that with a new {F#junior soror}{M#junior frater} on the Xianzhou, our research would have a worthy successor...

t_ch: 本来是挺开心的事，可惜老师他…哎…让你见笑了。哦对了，你是还有什么事要问吗？
t_en: Your arrival would have been a joy to us all, if not for Master's... Well... I'm sorry for being so depressed. What do you have for me this time?

t_ch: 研究的事，你还打算继续吗？
t_en: Are you going to continue your master's research?

t_ch: 老师所开启的课题，我恐怕难以为继。学会将另一项重任托付给了我。
t_en: I'm afraid I can't, as currently I'm occupied with another subject commissioned by the Guild.

t_ch: 我必须和天才俱乐部的那位「黑塔」大人进行接洽，成为她执笔巨著的责任编辑。客观地说，这是我们烛墨学派载入史册的大项目了。
t_en: I need to work with Herta of the Genius Society as the general editor for her new magnum opus. Objectively speaking, this project is of vital importance, and once it succeeds, we Candelagraphos will be recorded in history.

t_ch: 但这个课题是老师最后的心血…我再想想吧，或许能有个折中的方案。
t_en: But on the other hand, I can't just give up Master's project and his legacy... I need some time. Maybe I'll come up with a solution.

t_ch: 你之后有什么打算？
t_en: What are you going to do next?

t_ch: 我会暂时留在仙舟。但我不会继续老师研究，四处逛逛看看，感受下这里的风土人情也好。这么难得的机会，我可舍不得浪费。
t_en: Well, I'll stay on the Xianzhou ship, but instead of continuing Master's research, I'll experience the unique customs and culture here. I don't want to waste a precious opportunity.

t_ch: 你有所不知，学会收紧了在仙舟的课题预算，外派的名额也大幅度减少，来一趟可不容易。
t_en: Maybe you haven't heard about it. The Guild has cut its budget for field research and greatly reduced the number of scholars sent to other worlds. It's not easy to come here anymore.

t_ch: 在你看来，陶德是个什么样的人？
t_en: What do you think of Todd?

t_ch: 老实说，很难用短短几句话来评价他，于我而言，他是领我进入烛墨学派的老师，也是我在「智识」路途上的引路人。
t_en: It's hard to describe him in just a few words. For me, he was my mentor who brought me to the Candelagraphos and guided me to the Path of Erudition.

t_ch: 尽管老师的一些想法我无法理解，有时候我感觉他似乎有点太过执拗，因而显得有些不近人情了。但我知道他并无恶意，他只是个纯粹的学者。
t_en: Although I didn't always understand his ideas and sometimes he seemed too stubborn, I knew that he meant no harm, and that his aloofness was simply because he was a pure scholar.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: I see.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，若不是老师不在，我们现在估计早已成为同门了吧。
t_en: If Master were still here, {NICKNAME}, I guess we would have become peers by now.

t_ch: 我虽然不如老师那般饱学，但毕竟也是正式授衔的学士，你以后要是有什么感兴趣的问题，可以随时来和我聊聊。
t_en: While I'm nowhere near as erudite as Master, I'm an authorized scholar in any case. If you have any questions, please feel free to discuss them with me.

t_ch: 至于老师的事…算了，我还是先冷静冷静再想想怎么办吧。
t_en: As for Master's subject... Ah, forget it. I need to calm down, and perhaps I'll figure out something later.

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 幽囚重地，切勿徘徊…原来是{NICKNAME}大人，是在下失礼了。您再度造访幽囚狱，是有什么事情要办吗？
t_en: No loitering in The Shackling Prison! ...Ah, it's you, {NICKNAME}. My apologies, that was impolite of me. If I may ask, what brings you here to The Shackling Prison once more?

t_ch: 你认识我？
t_en: You know me?

t_ch: 哈哈！在下怎么会不认识您呢？呼雷越狱时，若是没有您的帮助，我恐怕也未必能活到今天啊。
t_en: Haha, how could I not? When Hoolay escaped from his cell, were it not for your help, I might not be standing here today — or anywhere else, for that matter.

t_ch: 其实也没什么事要办…
t_en: No particular reason...

t_ch: 您是罗浮的贵客，想去哪里都可以，轮不到在下说三道四，不过…这幽囚狱毕竟不是什么阳间地方，如果没有事情要办，您还是少来为好。
t_en: While it's not my place to comment... The Shackling Prison is, after all, not a place for the living. Even though you are an esteemed guest of the Luofu and are free to go wherever you wish, it may be advisable to avoid this place unless you have business here.

t_ch: 今次幽囚狱遭逢千年未有之重创，诸多事务亟待处理，如果您没有其他事情，请允许在下失陪。
t_en: The Shackling Prison has just taken a severe hit unlike anything else that's ever occurred in the past thousand years, and there is a surfeit of outstanding matters that I must tend to. If there's nothing else, please excuse me.

t_ch: 我有些问题想请教…
t_en: I have a few questions...

t_ch: 大人不必客气，请尽管发问。
t_en: Please, ask away.

t_ch: 幽囚狱现在情况如何？
t_en: How's The Shackling Prison faring?

t_ch: 唉…在之前的暴乱之中，那群步离人为了给自己打掩护，放出了不少囚犯。如今虽然暴乱平定，但仍有些漏网之鱼在狱中流窜。
t_en: *sigh* When the riot happened, the borisin set a good number of prisoners free and used them as decoys. Even though the riot has since been suppressed, there are still fugitives on the loose on prison grounds.

t_ch: 好在各部门的反应还算迅速，联通外界的出入口被及时封锁，关押恶逆的「无间区」与更深处的「黑绳区」也未受波及。
t_en: Thankfully, the various commissions took swift action to seal the portals connecting this prison to the outside world before anything worse could happen. This prevented the Avici Gaol, where evildoers are confined, and the Kalasutra Gaol located deeper within the prison from being thrown into chaos.

t_ch: 接下来无非是瓮中捉鳖罢了…唉，希望别再出什么岔子了。
t_en: With them trapped within the premises, all that is left to do now is to secure them one by one... Ugh, I hope nothing else goes awry.

t_ch: 平日工作很危险吧？
t_en: Is your job dangerous?

t_ch: 说来惭愧，这幽囚狱一直都是整个罗浮最安全的地方…在下平日里的工作非但谈不上危险，甚至可以说是无趣。
t_en: This is embarrassing to admit, but you could say that The Shackling Prison was the safest place in the Luofu all this time... Not only is my job in no way dangerous usually, but I even find the work somewhat dull and lifeless.

t_ch: 结果这一回可「有趣」了…唉，要不是有您和另外几位英雄在场，真不知道事情会如何收场……
t_en: And lo and behold, things got real "lively" this time around... If you and the other heroes hadn't been around, who knows what would have happened in the end?

t_ch: 你为什么会在十王司工作？
t_en: Why are you working for the Ten-Lords Commission?

t_ch: 在下算不上「在十王司工作」啦…只是被借调到幽囚狱，听从十王司指挥的云骑军而已。您大概也知道，协助十王司的工作，也是云骑军的义务之一。
t_en: I don't really work for them... I'm actually a Cloud Knight on secondment to The Shackling Prison, so I'm reporting to the Ten-Lords Commission until further notice. As you're probably aware, assisting the Ten-Lords Commission is very much also part of the Cloud Knights' duties.

t_ch: 不过大部分时候，幽囚狱里还是专业的人在做专业的事。比如这里关押的很多妖魔鬼怪，我看着都觉得害怕，更别提对付它们了……
t_en: For the most part, however, it's the professionals who do what they do best that keep the Shackling Prison running. For instance, you know how this place has scores of monsters and fiends locked up? I'm afraid of even looking at them, let alone fighting them...

t_ch: 遇到这种家伙，一般还是会交由判官和冥差处理——他们才是专业人士，我们云骑军只是从旁辅助罢了。
t_en: When I encounter such beings, I usually let the judges or spiritfarers handle them. After all, they are the true specialists... Us Cloud Knights simply provide aid wherever we can.

t_ch: 还有别的事，先不聊了。
t_en: I got to go. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 好的，请您保重。
t_en: Alright, take care.

t_ch: 还有别的事，先不聊了。
t_en: I got to go. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 好的，请您保重。
t_en: Alright, take care.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，别来无恙。您再度造访幽囚狱，是有什么事情要办吗？
t_en: Good day, {NICKNAME}. I trust you've been well? What brings you here again to The Shackling Prison? Official business?

t_ch: 客官好！我这「清玩斋」小本经营，童叟无欺！店里都是我精心准备的文玩物什，
t_en: Welcome to my Elegance Pagoda, dear customer! All the fandangles here are carefully prepared by myself.

t_ch: 客官你要是有什么感兴趣的，看看可以，别着急上手。
t_en: Please feel free to take a look if anything catches your attention. Just, please don't touch them.

t_ch: 什么意思？看不起人是吧？
t_en: What's that supposed to mean, huh?

t_ch: 不不不，在下绝不是这个意思。
t_en: I apologize if my words caused a misunderstanding. That's not what I meant.

t_ch: 你这文玩一定很贵吧！
t_en: These fandangles must be incredibly expensive, I guess?

t_ch: 不贵，我这都是小本买卖，贵不到哪里去，只讲究一个「眼缘」。
t_en: No, that is hardly the case. These curios are carefully prepared for those who are "destined" to become their new masters.

t_ch: 好古怪的规矩…
t_en: Your rules are really weird...

t_ch: 文玩千奇百怪，保存不易，这都是我们文玩圈子里的规矩，让您见笑了。
t_en: I apologize for the inconvenience, but these rules have been in place in our circle for decades, as reserving fandangles is always a painstaking endeavor.

t_ch: 有些文玩材质特殊，要恒温保存，又要保证湿度，诸如此类的讲究颇多，我这也才是不得已才如此待客。
t_en: For example, some of them must be kept at constant temperature and humidity, and different fandangles have different requirements for storage conditions. That's why I have to prohibit my clients from touching what we have worked so hard to preserve.

t_ch: 客官要是有合眼缘的物件，待会儿我端出来，找个敞亮的位置，带上放大镜和手套，这样好好把玩才尽兴。
t_en: If you so happen to be destined for one, I'll place it in a bright place and bring a magnifying glass and gloves to you. Then you can admire it to your heart's content.

t_ch: 怎么样，您有什么感兴趣的吗？
t_en: Has anything piqued your interest?

t_ch: 这文玩有什么特别之处吗？
t_en: What's so special about these fandangles?

t_ch: 倒也不是我吹，我家世代就干这个，无数个百年下来也算是有点眼力劲儿，因此挑出来摆上台面的件件都是精品。
t_en: It's not that I'm bragging, but my family has been in this business for generations. The experience we have passed down for many centuries has allowed me to find the best of the best.

t_ch: 像有些细节不够完美的，你别看有瑕疵，但这都是名家大师手工制作，莫说仙舟，便是放眼银河也是只此一件的绝版货。
t_en: Some fandangles may have flaws in details, but they are genuine products handcrafted by revered artisans and are unique in this Cosmos.

t_ch: 感觉你这店规矩好多…
t_en: You've set so many rules for your store...

t_ch: 让你见笑了，只是因为文玩保存不易，有些大师接的订单堆太多了，有些套件尾款一等就是几十年。
t_en: I apologize for the inconvenience, but reserving fandangles is always a painstaking endeavor, and some of them may need to be reserved here for decades before they are finally finished by artisans and delivered to customers.

t_ch: 不细心呵护，这些物件我怎么敢放心交到顾客手里呢。
t_en: How am I supposed to deliver these priceless treasures if I don't take good care of them?

t_ch: 你好像很讲究「眼缘」？
t_en: You seem to pay particular attention to "destiny."

t_ch: 店里的文玩都是以我的审美标准精挑细选出来的，我挂出来卖，自然也有几分求知音的意思。
t_en: All the fandangles here were carefully selected and arranged by myself. So naturally, I hope they will find their destined owners.

t_ch: 要是能有懂欣赏的主顾，或者是路过的游客恰巧有「眼缘」，生意归生意，聊几句也是件雅事。
t_en: If I happen to meet a customer who knows how to appreciate fandangles, or who is destined to become the new owner of my fandangles, it'll be a pleasure to do business or just exchange a few words with them.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好，要是有喜欢的可以跟我交流交流，这年头，同好难求啊。
t_en: Sure, feel free to come to me if anything catches your eye. It's not often to meet another curio lover these days.

t_ch: 看来店里的东西未必合你眼缘，没关系，以后你可以常来，等我进完货，说不定哪次你就遇到心头好了。
t_en: Nothing is destined to become yours this time, it seems. But don't worry, you can come here later and look around as much as you like, and perhaps one day you'll find something that hits the spot.

t_ch: 你要是闲来无事，可以来我这逛逛，放心，我不是要急着推销。
t_en: Feel free to look around here. Don't worry, I'm not in a haste to sell my fandangles.

t_ch: 我开这店主要是和同好交个朋友，你若真找到心头好了，价格都好说。
t_en: I opened this store mainly to make friends with fandangle lovers. If you find something you really like, I can give you the best price.

t_ch: 我有些问题想请教…
t_en: I have a few questions...

t_ch: 大人不必客气，请尽管发问。
t_en: Please, ask away.

t_ch: 幽囚狱现在情况如何？
t_en: How's The Shackling Prison faring?

t_ch: 唉…在之前的暴乱之中，那群步离人为了给自己打掩护，放出了不少囚犯。如今虽然暴乱平定，但仍有些漏网之鱼在狱中流窜。
t_en: *sigh* When the riot happened, the borisin set a good number of prisoners free and used them as decoys. Even though the riot has since been suppressed, there are still fugitives on the loose on prison grounds.

t_ch: 好在各部门的反应还算迅速，联通外界的出入口被及时封锁，关押恶逆的「无间区」与更深处的「黑绳区」也未受波及。
t_en: Thankfully, the various commissions took swift action to seal the portals connecting this prison to the outside world before anything worse could happen. This prevented the Avici Gaol, where evildoers are confined, and the Kalasutra Gaol located deeper within the prison from being thrown into chaos.

t_ch: 接下来无非是瓮中捉鳖罢了…唉，希望别再出什么岔子了。
t_en: With them trapped within the premises, all that is left to do now is to secure them one by one... Ugh, I hope nothing else goes awry.

t_ch: 平日工作很危险吧？
t_en: Is your job dangerous?

t_ch: 说来惭愧，这幽囚狱一直都是整个罗浮最安全的地方…在下平日里的工作非但谈不上危险，甚至可以说是无趣。
t_en: This is embarrassing to admit, but you could say that The Shackling Prison was the safest place in the Luofu all this time... Not only is my job in no way dangerous usually, but I even find the work somewhat dull and lifeless.

t_ch: 结果这一回可「有趣」了…唉，要不是有您和另外几位英雄在场，真不知道事情会如何收场……
t_en: And lo and behold, things got real "lively" this time around... If you and the other heroes hadn't been around, who knows what would have happened in the end?

t_ch: 你为什么会在十王司工作？
t_en: Why are you working for the Ten-Lords Commission?

t_ch: 在下算不上「在十王司工作」啦…只是被借调到幽囚狱，听从十王司指挥的云骑军而已。您大概也知道，协助十王司的工作，也是云骑军的义务之一。
t_en: I don't really work for them... I'm actually a Cloud Knight on secondment to The Shackling Prison, so I'm reporting to the Ten-Lords Commission until further notice. As you're probably aware, assisting the Ten-Lords Commission is very much also part of the Cloud Knights' duties.

t_ch: 不过大部分时候，幽囚狱里还是专业的人在做专业的事。比如这里关押的很多妖魔鬼怪，我看着都觉得害怕，更别提对付它们了……
t_en: For the most part, however, it's the professionals who do what they do best that keep the Shackling Prison running. For instance, you know how this place has scores of monsters and fiends locked up? I'm afraid of even looking at them, let alone fighting them...

t_ch: 遇到这种家伙，一般还是会交由判官和冥差处理——他们才是专业人士，我们云骑军只是从旁辅助罢了。
t_en: When I encounter such beings, I usually let the judges or spiritfarers handle them. After all, they are the true specialists... Us Cloud Knights simply provide aid wherever we can.

t_ch: 还有别的事，先不聊了。
t_en: I got to go. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 好的，请您保重。
t_en: Alright, take care.

t_ch: 还有别的事，先不聊了。
t_en: I got to go. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 好的，请您保重。
t_en: Alright, take care.

t_ch: 是你？「清玩斋」无任欢迎。
t_en: Oh, it's you again? Welcome to Elegance Pagoda.

t_ch: 你自己随便挑吧，要是有什么感兴趣的，看看可以，别着急上手。
t_en: Feel free to take a look at everything here. Just please don't touch anything.

t_ch: 能给我看看你的货吗？
t_en: Could you show me your fandangles?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
t_en: Has business been going well lately?

t_ch: 生意顺不顺遂，我倒是没什么所谓。不过托你的福，这金人巷越来越人声鼎沸，也算替我打开了广交朋友的大门啊。
t_en: I don't really care about the business. Still, the Aurum Alley has become busier and busier because of you, and this has helped me tremendously in expanding my connections.

t_ch: 美中不足就是有些客人，也不知道是什么地方来的，特别不守我这的规矩…我赶他走，他还不乐意，我还落埋怨。心累啊。
t_en: The downside is, there are customers who don't follow the rules here, and I don't even know where they come from... When I ask them to leave, they become upset and start complaining. I'm tired of things like that.

t_ch: 不过总体来说，还是挺好的。最近认识了几个域外来的客人，谈吐和学养都不错。
t_en: Overall, things are positive. The Outworld customers that I've befriended lately are all decent and well-mannered.

t_ch: 他们都说，是听说了你们复兴金人巷的故事，才专程过来游览的。你让我认识了几位非常值得交游的朋友，我打心底里向你表示感谢。
t_en: They said they decided to come here after listening to the revival story of the Aurum Alley. I've made some nice friends because of you, and I really appreciate that.

t_ch: 瞧一瞧看一看！今天不把杂技看，余生千年留遗憾！吞火球、踏燃碳，空中飞人大家赞！四方的宾朋来围观，个个都说——来了悠姐杂技团，给我千金也不换！
t_en: Hear ye! Hear ye! Enjoy acrobatic shows for a tiny fee! Come to Ms. Yo's acrobatic troupe and take a seat! Breathing fire, walking on burning coal, and thrilling trapeze acts for you to see! Let go of your burdens, and relax yourself in glee!

t_ch: 这位化外民小朋友，要不要来看看杂技啊？
t_en: Want to enjoy an acrobatic show, my Outworlder friend?

t_ch: 杂技是什么？
t_en: What's acrobatics?

t_ch: 哟，你没看过杂技吗？那可得来悠姐这里看看了。
t_en: Oh, you've never enjoyed an acrobatic show before? Then you should definitely watch one right here!

t_ch: 你这都有什么节目？
t_en: What kind of shows do you have?

t_ch: 我这节目可多着呢，你听我说与你听啊——
t_en: You're asking the right person! I have a whole bunch of shows for you!

t_ch: 钻火圈、拿大顶，徒手油锅捞铁饼。舞毒蛇、耍老鹰，能言走兽当笑星。总而言之一句话——只有看官不敢看的，没有我们不敢演的。
t_en: Fire hoops and handstands you may already know, but we have so many other stunts untold. Snake dance and eagle show, and even the fiercest beasts will dance to our tempo. In short, we provide any performances, as long as you have the guts to enjoy them.

t_ch: 不过现在还没到演出的时候，悠姐可以陪你唠唠。小朋友，有什么事吗？
t_en: But it's not time for the show yet, so I'm here chatting with you. How can I help you, my young friend?

t_ch: 你问呗，只要姐答得上来就行。
t_en: I'll answer your questions to the best of my ability.

t_ch: 你们平时在哪里演出？
t_en: Where do you usually perform?

t_ch: 我们杂技团把总部放到了金人巷，不过平时总要去各地演出，有时候还要去其他仙舟上演出，所以除非逢年过节，不然也不搞剧场，大家都撂地摆摊，各凭本事。
t_en: Although our headquarters are in Aurum Alley, more often than not, we travel all over to perform, and sometimes we even go to other Xianzhou ships. So, we rarely perform in theaters unless there's a big festival or something. Most of the time, we perform on the street and manage our business independently.

t_ch: 其实撂地和剧场各有各的好处。剧场里能整更大的设备，搞点空中飞人什么的杂技，但撂地气氛好啊，大家都围在一起看，演好演坏都能立刻得到反馈，多热闹。
t_en: Both performing on the street and in the theater have their benefits. Theaters allow us to set up large-sized equipment for trapeze or other complex acrobatic acts, but street performances are much livelier, and you'll get immediate feedback from the audience.

t_ch: 你要是在金人巷看到有杂耍卖艺的，那不用想，就是我悠姐的杂技团。要是觉得好呢，你就过去扫码给点赏钱，要是觉得不好呢…你就来这跟姐说，姐去教育他们！
t_en: Whenever you see jugglers in Aurum Alley, they are from my troupe. If you find their performance satisfactory, feel free to tip them, and if you don't... just let me know and I'll reprimand them!

t_ch: 为什么要开杂技团呢？
t_en: Why did you start your acrobatic troupe?

t_ch: 为什么？为了赚钱呗。「杂技」在仙舟可是相当受欢迎的，和幻戏比都不遑多让呢。
t_en: To make money, of course! Acrobatic shows are almost as popular as immersia on Xianzhou ships.

t_ch: 道理也蛮简单的——仙舟人少说也要活个两三百年，难免活着活着就觉得没趣儿了。
t_en: That's easy to understand, right? Anyone who has lived for two or three centuries will eventually find their lives boring.

t_ch: 这种时候，看着别人像活腻了似的，在满地的利刃上面倒挂金钩…这帮观众就一边留着冷汗一边产生了「活着」的真实感。
t_en: So they will watch others dance on the razor's edge... Such thrilling performances will refill them with the feeling of being alive.

t_ch: 不过说实在的，无论是我手下的那些艺人，还是你姐我，也都是因为这样才入行的。
t_en: To be honest, that's also why my peers and I joined this industry.

t_ch: 人活太久了就容易麻木，越是麻木就越需要刺激…不过和你说这些，你大概也不明白，估计只会觉得是「仙舟人活太久以至于活腻歪了」吧？哈哈哈。
t_en: Those who live too long tend to become numb, and that's why they need constant stimulation... Well, I don't think you'd really understand how we feel. You probably just think "these guys have lived too long to say 'no' to their deathwish." Hahahaha!

t_ch: 你认识桂乃芬吗？
t_en: Do you know Guinaifen?

t_ch: 桂乃芬？哦哦，小桂子姐当然认识啦！那可是我们杂技团的当家摇钱树啊！
t_en: Guinaifen? Oh, you mean Little Gui? Of course, I do! She's our best performer and moneymaker!

t_ch: 你别看这小妮子是个短生种，业务水平可不比我们任何一个长生种要差。有时候做的那些狠活啊…姐看着都觉得害怕。
t_en: She might be a short-life species, but she's as excellent an acrobat as any long-life ones! Sometimes her stunts are so thrilling that... they even send chills down my spine.

t_ch: 姐一直想问她：「短生种一辈子不过百年，为什么要一直做这种危险的演出呢？」但…这话姐这个做老板的不适合问出口啊…哈哈哈。
t_en: I've always wanted to ask her. "Even if you led a peaceful life, you would have less than a century to live. Why are you so dedicated to such dangerous shows?" But... as you know, since I'm her boss, I can't just ask her like things that... Hahaha.

t_ch: 姐个人猜想啊，正因为小桂子一辈子不过百年，她才会比我们这些几百年的老帮菜更需要让自己的生命散发出光热…当然啦，这都是姐瞎猜的。
t_en: Perhaps it's because her life is short that she wants to live every moment to the fullest... but of course, this is just my speculation.

t_ch: 不过还是要说…虽然小桂子杂技水平很强，但最近对本职工作越来越不上心了，开始沉迷搞什么网络直播，搞什么「整活儿」…唉，年轻人终究是年轻人。
t_en: Still, I want to remind her... Despite her excellent acrobatic skills, she has recently slacked off on her performance, shifting her focus to live streaming and garish bells and whistles... Well, she's still so young after all...

t_ch: 你最近要是见到了她，也帮我敲打敲打她啊。就和她说：年轻人热衷于新鲜事物没关系，但千万别忘了本职工作才是你的立身之本。
t_en: If you run into her, don't forget to pass on my words to her. It's okay for young people to try new and fancy stuff, but they must not neglect what keeps them afloat.

t_ch: 没别的事了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 好嘞。那有空来看姐的演出啊～
t_en: Sure thing. Don't forget to come over and enjoy my performance when you have time!

t_ch: 瞧一瞧看一看！今天不把杂技看，余生千年留遗憾！吞火球、踏燃碳，空中飞人大家赞！四方的宾朋来围观，个个都说——来了悠姐杂技团，给我千金也不换！
t_en: Hear ye! Hear ye! Enjoy acrobatic shows for a tiny fee! Come to Ms. Yo's acrobatic troupe and take a seat! Breathing fire, walking on burning coal, and thrilling trapeze acts for you to see! Let go of your burdens, and relax yourself in glee!

t_ch: 嗯？这不是{NICKNAME}小朋友吗？来来来，节目还没开始，陪姐聊会天。
t_en: Hmmm? If it isn't my little friend {NICKNAME}? The show has not started yet. Come on over and chat with me.

t_ch: 有些事想问你。
t_en: I have a few questions I wish to ask you...

t_ch: 没别的事了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 好的。请你闭上双眼，在心中默念自己所问之事。
t_en: I see. Please close your eyes and meditate on the question in your mind.

t_ch: 你今天已经卜过一卦了。「一日不二卦」，请明天再来吧。
t_en: I have already divined for you today. Please come back tomorrow.

t_ch: 我想卜算一下凶吉祸福。
t_en: I wish to know my fortune.

t_ch: 没有其他想问的事儿了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 眼观鼻鼻观心，你在心中默念着自己所问之事。
t_en: You dive into the depths of your mind and meditate on the question in your mind.

t_ch: 玉兆开始转动，某种看不见的屏障隔绝了你与外界的联系。在突如其来的宁静里，某个模糊的画面渐渐清晰了起来——
t_en: As the jade abacus starts spinning, some kind of invisible barrier separates you from the outside world. In the midst of the sudden tranquility, a blurred yet unmistakable image gradually becomes clear...

t_ch: 你看见的是仙舟「曜青」，它挟风驭雷，自九天之外疾驰而来。甲板上无数狐人披甲站立，威风凛凛势不可挡。
t_en: You are seeing the Xianzhou Yaoqing, which is advancing at a dazzling speed, carrying the momentum of tempest and thunder. Countless foxians, fully armed, are standing on the deck in a majestic posture.

t_ch: 此象得主英武无双，勇气过人。对于你所问之事——你拥有过人的意志力，保持积极进取的心态，披荆斩棘不畏险阻方有所得。切勿因急于求成而盲目屈从，切记做人做事需有始有终。
t_en: The bearer of this sign possesses unparalleled courage. As for the question in your mind — stay positive and brave forward, and you will overcome all difficulties with your unwavering willpower. Do not blindly follow others because you are in a hurry to achieve your goals. Make sure you always finish what you start.

t_ch: 你看见的是仙舟「罗浮」，云骑军拱卫着遮天蔽日的建木，化外民登舟求访，星槎海中人潮汹涌声浪滔天。
t_en: You are seeing the Xianzhou Luofu, on which the towering Ambrosial Arbor looms over countless Cloud Knights. With the arrival of numerous Outworlders, Starskiff Haven is alive with people from all walks of life.

t_ch: 此象得主生有一颗玲珑心，四海之内皆朋友。对于你所问之事——你所拥有的人脉超乎常人想象，凡事皆有他人助力，虽有奔波感但诸事可成。切勿报以悲观态度处事，切记说话三思不可恶语伤人。
t_en: The bearer of this sign is born with a gentle soul and can easily make friends throughout the world. As for the question in your mind — your connections are far stronger than anyone can imagine, and your friends will help you out despite the twists and turns. Stay positive and be careful with what your words in case they may hurt others.

t_ch: 你看见的是仙舟「方壶」，持明游荡于方寸烟海之中，「护珠人」静默凝思，隐隐有肃杀的龙吟声传来。
t_en: You are seeing the Xianzhou Fanghu, with Vidyadhara roaming around the Miniature Myriads and Pearlkeepers meditating. The sound of belligerent dragon chants echoes through space from time to time.

t_ch: 此象得主身居高位，刚正不阿。对于你所问之事——你心向正义，行事魄力十足，只需遵从内心良知道德，名利唾手可得。然而高处不胜寒，切勿封闭内心与朋友断交，行事多为亲友考虑，切记欲速则不达。
t_en: The bearer of this sign is in a high position and has a righteous soul. As for the question in your mind — just follow your conscience and you will gain fame and fortune. However, your journey may be a lonely one. Do not sever your ties with your family and friends and try to keep them in mind. Remember, haste makes waste.

t_ch: 你看见的是仙舟「玉阙」，观星士掌瞰云镜窥向星海深处，玉兆转动不停，于阴霾之中指引众人驶向光亮彼岸。
t_en: You are seeing the Xianzhou Yuque. The stargazers steer the Cloudpeer Telescope and peer into the depths of the stars. In the midst of the gloom, the spinning jade abacus emanates a radiance, guiding the ship to its bright destination.

t_ch: 此象得主生有宿慧，伶俐聪明。对于你所问之事——世事纷杂不堪，你需保持内心的宁静，遵从内心所想前行便可得。你一路走来所见的幽谷深涧为常人难寻之景，切勿随波逐流乱了内心方向，切记孤独是开拓者的必经之道。
t_en: The bearer of this sign is gifted with wisdom. As for the question in your mind — keep your inner peace and follow your heart. You have experienced darkness that ordinary people do not see. Do not let that turmoil cloud your sight, for solitude is a quintessential part of those who trailblaze.

t_ch: 你看见的是仙舟「朱明」，匠人于此熔金铄铁，洪炉昼夜不停吞地吞吐烈焰，甲板上络绎不绝地运输着各式机巧。
t_en: You are seeing the Xianzhou Zhuming, on which countless craftsmen are busy melting metals and forging their masterpieces. The ship's blast furnaces are smoldering with flames, and various ingenia are constantly being transported on its deck.

t_ch: 此象得主心灵手巧，极具创造力。对于你所问之事——你将见到全新的光景，此事为你囊中物。运用你过往的智慧与经验，诸事可得且有意外之喜。切勿思虑过多优柔寡断，需防备生人，切记多动脑，凡事莫要想当然。
t_en: The bearer of this sign is handy and creative. As for the question in your mind — your fate will lead you to new insights. Use your wisdom and experience, and you will be able to overcome difficulties and get pleasant surprises. Do not overthink or hesitate. Be wary of strangers. Always rely on your own wisdom and do not take anything for granted.

t_ch: 你看见的是仙舟「虚陵」，其他仙舟于此渡航流转，除此以外再不见人烟，仅有金人星罗密布。
t_en: You are seeing the Xianzhou Xuling, a mobile dock that provides supplies and maintenance services for other Xianzhou ships for supply and maintenance. This ship is filled with aurumatons, but not living beings.

t_ch: 此象得主意志坚定，勤勉向上。对于你所问之事——此事需投入大量的准备工作，但你的辛勤皆有回报，更上一层楼，楼上风光无限好。切勿自怨自艾，勿要固执己见，切记有耕耘必有收获。
t_en: The bearer of this sign is determined and diligent. As for the question in your mind — this matter will require a lot of preparation, but your diligence will eventually pay off. Only by reaching the peak can one get the best view. Do not complain about the world, and do not be too strongly opinionated. Have faith in your efforts, because they will bear fruit.

t_ch: 天呐，药王慈怀！我没认错的话，你就是那位{NICKNAME}吧？
t_en: By the mercy of Sanctus Medicus! If I'm not mistaken, you must be {NICKNAME}, right?

t_ch: 这鬼地方怎么人人都认识我？
t_en: Why does everybody in this hellhole seem to know who I am?

t_ch: 这很正常啦～幽囚狱本质上是个熟人社会嘛，消息传播很快的。
t_en: That's to be expected. Everyone knows everyone in The Shackling Prison, so word travels fast around here.

t_ch: 你认识我？不会是我抓的你吧？
t_en: You know me? I didn't throw you in there, did I?

t_ch: 我哪有那个荣幸被你抓啊？幽囚狱本质上是个熟人社会嘛，消息传播很快的。
t_en: I wish! Now that'd have been something to boast about. Everyone knows everyone in The Shackling Prison, so word travels fast around here.

t_ch: 你大概还不认识我，容我自我介绍一下吧！我以前是「药王秘传」的一名莳者，现在是阿合马铁窗集团的首席技术研发官。
t_en: You probably don't know who I am. Let me introduce myself. I'm a former disciple of Sanctus Medicus and the current Chief Technical R&D Officer of the Arma Correctional Group.

t_ch: 平时呢，我就帮着阿合马研发一些新产品…我自己也卖些药品，你要是感兴趣的话，可以找我购买～
t_en: I'm usually busy developing new products for Arma, but I also sell medicines in a personal capacity. If you're ever in need of medicine, don't hesitate to come to me!

t_ch: 顺便一说，我们集团都是不收现金的，仅支持「奇巧零食」付款～
t_en: And before I forget, the Correctional Group doesn't take monetary payments, only Trick Snacks.

t_ch: 你卖的药品合法吗？
t_en: Are your medicines licit?

t_ch: 你跑到我这儿买合法药品，是不是有点大可不必了？
t_en: Don't you find this place a little too out of the way to travel to just for licit medicines?

t_ch: 但是放心好啦！我卖的东西绝对无害！不然赤衣大人也不会允许我在这里搞小研究，对吧？
t_en: That said, you needn't worry. My goods are absolutely safe for consumption! Madam Chiyi would never greenlight my research in here otherwise.

t_ch: 你们竟敢要我的奇巧零食？
t_en: How dare you covet my Trick Snacks!

t_ch: 这东西在你手里只是普通的零食，在我们手里可是一般等价物啊！我都知道，你随随便便就能合成几千个奇巧零食！换句话说，你可是掌握了幽囚狱金融体系的发币权啊！
t_en: Those are just boring old snacks to you, but I'll have you know that they're no different from money to us! I also know that you can synthesize thousands of Trick Snacks with a flick of your wrist... In other words, you hold the reins of The Shackling Prison's entire financial system!

t_ch: 这要是不行使你的权力，不是亏大发了？
t_en: Think of all the profits you could be making if you leveraged that!

t_ch: 如何？要买点什么支持一下集团的生意吗？
t_en: So, how about buying something and supporting the Correctional Group?

t_ch: 给我看看货吧…
t_en: Show me the goods...

t_ch: 想问你点事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 问吧问吧～不过别聊太久，手头还有点事没结～
t_en: Ask away! I can't chat long, though. I still have some matters to take care of.

t_ch: 当初为什么加入药王秘传？
t_en: Why did you join the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus back then?

t_ch: 主要是因为在丹鼎司，正常研究实在太束手束脚了嘛！想要复现一些典籍中记录的生物技术和古代药方，得走层层审批～流程繁琐的要命～
t_en: Mainly because there were too many restrictions around conducting research at the Alchemy Commission. I had to navigate layers and layers of red tape just to replicate some biotech processes and ancient prescriptions recorded in tomes. It's so bureaucratic!

t_ch: 但加入药王秘传后，很多事情就变得简单多了～有了药王秘传这层地下关系网，连请假的审批流程都快了很多～
t_en: But after I became a disciple, things became much less complicated. After I gained access to the underground connections afforded by the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, even the approval process for taking time off work was much quicker!

t_ch: 但是我现在已经反省了！真的！我现在只想本本分分做生意，跟我兄弟阿合马和白鸟一起，把铁窗集团做大做强！
t_en: I've turned over a new leaf since, though... No, really! My only goal now is to be an honest businessman alongside Arma and Shiratori. We'll grow the Correctional Group into as large and powerful a business as we possibly can!

t_ch: 到那时候，在这幽囚狱中继续进行以前的研究，也不是不可能…嘿嘿嘿～
t_en: When that happens, I may even be able to pick up where I left off and continue my research here at the Shackling Prison, hehehe.

t_ch: 首席技术研发官是做什么的？
t_en: What are your responsibilities as the Chief Technical R&D Officer?

t_ch: 阿合马擅长用监狱里的小垃圾，制造出能大大提升监狱生活品质的产品。他确实心灵手巧，搞些小手工绰绰有余，但也就仅限于搞些小手工了。
t_en: Arma excels at making products that can greatly improve one's carceral quality of life out of the trash lying about in prison. While he's no doubt a deft hand at creating trinkets, that's really all that he's good at.

t_ch: 而我不一样，我是个丹士啊～你看到厨余垃圾，我看到牙膏、沐浴液、护肤品…你看到蛇虫鼠蚁，我看到药品、滋补品、小零食……
t_en: Not me, though. I'm an alchemist! Where you see kitchen scraps and trash, I see toothpaste, body wash, skincare products, and more. Where you see pests and irritants, I see medicines, supplements, snacks...

t_ch: 幽囚狱里素材虽多，但设备有限。很多东西在外面稀松平常，但在这里可是稀罕玩意儿。以我的大才做这些日用化学品绰绰有余～
t_en: We have an abundance of raw materials in the Shackling Prison, but there's also an appalling lack of equipment and facilities here. Many things you wouldn't even take a second look at outside are rarities in here, and making these simple household chemical products is but child's play to me!

t_ch: 最初我自己做了一些牙膏，阿合马从里面看到了商机，就索性拉我入伙了。
t_en: All I made in the beginning was toothpaste, but when Arma noticed what I did, he spotted a business opportunity and roped me into his venture.

t_ch: 我给集团开发了很多热门商品～比如说仙人快乐茶口味的牙膏、燃茶口味的辣椒酱、可冲泡的苏打豆汁……
t_en: Since then, I have developed many bestsellers for the Correctional Group, such as Immortal's Delight-flavored toothpaste, Flaming Tea-flavored hot sauce, instant mung bean soda...

t_ch: 我的产品可都是我亲自试验过的，绝对有品质保障！当然，你要是吃坏肚子了，我这还有解毒止泻的药，免费供应，嘿嘿～
t_en: I personally try out all my products, so rest assured I can attest to their quality! Of course, if your stomach does become upset, I can supply you with detoxification and anti-diarrheal medication on the house, hehe.

t_ch: 你真的只打算做生意吗？
t_en: Is the business really your sole focus now?

t_ch: 你这家伙很会洞察人心嘛～当然不是了。人活一世，总要保留点梦想，对吧？
t_en: You're perceptive, aren't you? Well, the answer is no. Since we only have one life to live, we shouldn't give up our hopes and dreams so easily, don't you think?

t_ch: 我毕竟是个丹士，就算永陷幽囚深狱，也不可能就这样甘愿一辈子手搓日用化学品。
t_en: I'm an alchemist, after all. Even if I'm doomed to eternal confinement within the Shackling Prison, there's no way I'd be willing to resign myself to a lifetime of making household chemical products by hand.

t_ch: 我在想，也许有些学术上的谜题，反而在幽囚狱里才更容易搞清楚…比如…「起源长生者」的秘密～
t_en: I've been wondering... Perhaps certain academic conundrums can only be solved more easily if one is incarcerated in the Shackling Prison. For instance... the secrets of the Primus Immortus?

t_ch: 哎呀呀～我刚好像说了些不该说的话…就麻烦你当做没听见吧，谢啦！
t_en: Oops, I might have said a bit too much. Can we pretend we never had this conversation? Hehe, thanks!

t_ch: 没其他事了，你去忙吧。
t_en: That's all from me. I'll leave you to work then.

t_ch: 嘿嘿～阿合马铁窗集团竭诚为您服务～
t_en: Hehe, the Arma Correctional Group is glad to be of service!

t_ch: 没其他事了，那你去忙吧。
t_en: That's all from me. I'll leave you to work then.

t_ch: 嘿嘿～阿合马铁窗集团竭诚为您服务～
t_en: Hehe, the Arma Correctional Group is glad to be of service!

t_ch: 药王慈怀～助我发财～你又来啦？要买点什么阿合马铁窗集团研发的好东西吗？
t_en: O, mercy of Sanctus Medicus, please bless me with riches... Oh, you're here again. Any premium products developed by the Arma Correctional Group for you today?

t_ch: 你看见的是仙舟「苍城」，仅仅是只鳞片角便已遮天蔽日。飞速行驶中，它与噬界罗睺相撞……
t_en: You are seeing the Xianzhou Cangcheng. Even a corner of it is colossal enough to block all the stars and darken the sky. It is flying at a dazzling speed and then collides into Rahu, Devourer of Worlds...

t_ch: 此象得主明知不可为而为之，身陷囹吾而不自知。对于你所问之事——这或许会是一次惨烈的打击，你注定要承受这突如其来的灾难。世事无常，做好心理准备。
t_en: The bearer of this sign is unaware of their predicament and is attempting something that is doomed to fail. As for the question in your mind — this could be a devastating blow for you, and you are destined to suffer this unexpected disaster. Be prepared for the terrible end that fate will throw at you.

t_ch: 想问你点事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 问吧问吧～不过别聊太久，手头还有点事没结～
t_en: Ask away! I can't chat long, though. I still have some matters to take care of.

t_en: Hehe, the Arma Correctional Group is glad to be of service!

t_ch: 你看见的是仙舟「圆峤」，舟中居民面有愤恨之情，昔日亲友倒戈为战，混乱中「圆峤」失控，于红巨星的光芒笼罩中渐渐消失……
t_en: You are seeing the Xianzhou Yuanqiao, whose residents are consumed by rage, and friends turn against each other. In the midst of chaos, the Xianzhou Yuanqiao loses control and is engulfed in the flames of a red giant star...

t_ch: 此象得主是好多管闲事，易卷入纷争之中。对于你所问之事——此事毫无转圜余地，破镜难圆，你注定要见证这分离与破碎之景。需知大难临头各自飞是人之常情，勿要沉溺于往事之中。
t_en: The bearer of this sign is a busybody who always gets themselves into strife. As for the question in your mind — there is no turning back, and separation is inevitable. Please understand that it is common for people to abandon others just to find a way to keep themselves alive in the face of disaster. Don't dwell too much on your past.

t_ch: 你看见一个灵动的身影，在仙舟某处别院中呼朋引伴，与众人齐乐乐融融地玩着帝垣琼玉。
t_en: You are seeing a swift figure happily playing Celestial Jade with friends in a house somewhere on the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 此象得主远离尘世纷扰，悠闲自得。对于你所问之事——不必求而有所得，刻意追求反落了下乘。活在当下寻欢作乐，便自有福缘傍身。切勿为升斗米而折腰，切记功名利禄皆为身外物，平平淡淡才是真。
t_en: The bearer of this sign is far away from worries and living a carefree life. As for the question in your mind — do not deliberately pursue anything. Live in the moment and everything will be fine. Do not go against your nature for fame and fortune, because these are not what really matters.

t_ch: 你看见一白发男子正在卧榻上酣睡，几只小鸟正在窗棂并排而立，窗外春明景和，正是一片好风景。
t_en: You are seeing a white-haired man napping on a day bed. Several birds are standing side by side on the window pane through which lovely spring sun is streaming in.

t_ch: 此象得主总能偷得浮生半日闲。对于你所问之事——时机未到，且耐心等候。近期心神倦乏需闭目静养，注意保暖。切勿熬夜伤身，勿要为琐事动气，切记早睡早起身体好
t_en: The bearer of this sign can always find leisure time. As for the question in your mind — now is not yet the opportune moment. Stay patient and bide your time. If you have been feeling tired lately, calm your mind and rest well. Do not stay up late and do not get angry about trivial matters. Sleep early and live a healthy life.

t_ch: 你看见的是仙舟「岱舆」，它与视肉为战，终结了无数孽物的灾患，它虽坠落，但有新生正在破碎的摇篮里萌芽。
t_en: You see the Xianzhou Daiyu combating against the viscorpi and ending the cataclysm of countless abominations. Though it had fallen, new life now blooms from that shattered cradle.

t_ch: 此象得主身处黑夜之中，不见光亮。对于你所问之事——夜明之前，伸手不见五指，一切化为泡影。但你已处在天明之前，昨日之事不可留，明日的希望就在你心中。切记不要放弃希望。
t_en: The receiver of this omen is currently in thick darkness devoid of light. As for the question you asked — this darkness shall remain thick and impenetrable until the sun rises, and all shall fade as a shadow. However, you are near the break of dawn. The bygone worries of yesterday should not be kept, and the hope for tomorrow beats still in your heart. Remember not to give up on that hope.

t_ch: 是{NICKNAME}啊，刚想小憩片刻，你就来了。
t_en: You're here, {NICKNAME}. I was just about to take a break.

t_ch: 是想同我聊聊开拓者的列车冒险，还是想和我商讨在仙舟遇上的事情？
t_en: Do you want to talk about the adventure on the Trailblazer Express or the incidents that happened on Xianzhou?

t_ch: 不耽误将军的工作吗？
t_en: Won't I be holding you up?

t_ch: 无妨无妨，神策府的公务是永远处理不完的，就让青镞再等一等好了。
t_en: No worries. The work at the Seat of Divine Foresight is endless and will never be finished. Qingzu can wait.

t_ch: 何况你是仙舟盟友，来到神策府，岂有被晾在一边的道理？
t_en: Besides, you're a pivotal Xianzhou ally. How can I give you the cold shoulder when you're here?

t_ch: 看起来将军聊兴很高啊？
t_en: Looks like you are in the mood to talk.

t_ch: 待在神策府里批阅几个时辰的公文，你就会像我一样期望能有人聊聊天。
t_en: After poring over documents for hours here, I bet you would be the same.

t_ch: 青镞平日里是个说话逗趣的人，可惜她只要站在这儿对我开口，就是正正经经的报告腔，条分缕析，字字不离数据，乏味得紧。
t_en: Qingzu is fun to be around when she's not working, so it's a shame that she always speaks very seriously to me here. Her analytical and no-nonsense tone can be a bit dry.
